heading: Trust funds
label: 1321 Trust funds
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised SectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)1321(a)31:725s(a)(1st sentence, cls. (1)–(84)), (c).(less (a)(last sentence last proviso)), ; , §§ 1(a), 2(a), , 153; , , .June 26, 1934, ch. 756, § 2048 Stat. 1233June 15, 1943, ch. 12557 Stat. 152Pub. L. 94–502, § 40790 Stat. 2397Oct. 15, 19761321(b)31:725s(a)(2d sentence, last sentence 1st, 2d provisos).In the section, the cross-references to subsection (b) in the source provisions being restated are assumed to be references to clauses (1)–(84) of subsection (a) because the source provisions contain no subsection (b).In subsection (a), the words “appearing on the books of the Government” and “on the books of the Treasury” are omitted as surplus.In subsection (b), the words “effective ” and the 2d proviso are omitted as executed.July 1, 1935

References in TextThe Longshore and Harbor Workers’ Compensation Act, referred to in subsec. (a)(12), is , , which is classified generally to chapter 18 (§ 901 et seq.) of Title 33, Navigation and Navigable Waters. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act Mar. 4, 1927, ch. 50944 Stat. 1424section 901 of Title 33The International Boundary Commission, United States and Mexico, referred to in subsec. (a)(41), was redesignated the International Boundary and Water Commission, United States and Mexico, by the Water Treaty of 1944.The National Capital Park and Planning Commission, referred to in subsec. (a)(53), was abolished and its functions transferred to the National Capital Planning Commission by section 9 of , as added by , , which was classified to section 71h of former Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works, and was repealed and reenacted as , Public Buildings, Property, and Works, by , §§ 1, 6(b), , , 1304.act June 6, 1924, ch. 270act July 19, 1952, ch. 949, § 166 Stat. 790section 8711(f) of Title 40Pub. L. 107–217116 Stat. 1062Aug. 21, 2002The Rock Creek and Potomac Parkway Commission, referred to in subsec. (a)(54), was abolished and its functions transferred to the Office of National Parks, Buildings, and Reservations, Department of the Interior, by Ex. Ord. No. 6166, § 2, , set out as a note under , Government Organization and Employees. The name of the Office of National Parks, Buildings, and Reservations was changed to the “National Park Service” by , .June 10, 1933section 901 of Title 5act March 2, 1934, ch. 3848 Stat. 389The District of Columbia Workmen’s Compensation Act, referred to in subsec. (a)(57), probably means the District of Columbia Unemployment Compensation Act, , , which is not classified to the Code.act Aug. 28, 1935, ch. 79449 Stat. 946

Amendments2004—Subsec. (a)(59). added par. (59).Pub. L. 108–1991998—Subsec. (a)(92) to (94). struck out pars. (92) to (94) which read as follows:Pub. L. 105–261“(92) Fisher House Trust Fund, Department of the Army.“(93) Fisher House Trust Fund, Department of the Air Force.“(94) Fisher House Trust Fund, Department of the Navy.”1996—Subsec. (a)(92), (93). , added pars. (92) and (93).Pub. L. 104–106, § 914(c)(1)Subsec. (a)(94). , added par. (94).Pub. L. 104–201, § 1008(c)(1)Subsec. (b). , designated existing provisions as par. (1), substituted “Except as provided in paragraph (2), amounts accruing to these funds” for “Amounts accruing to these funds (except to the trust fund ‘Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund’)”, struck out “Expenditures from the trust fund ‘Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund’ shall be made only under annual appropriations and only if the appropriations are specifically authorized by law.” after second sentence, and added par. (2).Pub. L. 104–106, § 914(c)(2)Subsec. (b)(2)(D). , added subpar. (D).Pub. L. 104–201, § 1008(c)(2)1994—Subsec. (a)(91). added par. (91).Pub. L. 103–3221991—Subsec. (a)(45), (74), (82), (83). substituted “Department of Veterans Affairs” for “Veterans’ Administration”, wherever appearing.Pub. L. 102–541990—Subsec. (a)(5). , substituted “Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund” for “Personal funds of deceased inmates, Naval Home”.Pub. L. 101–510, § 1533(c)(1)(A)(i)Subsec. (a)(59), (81). , struck out pars. (59) “Soldiers’ Home, permanent fund.” and (81) “Soldiers’ Home, interest account.”Pub. L. 101–510, § 1533(c)(1)(A)(ii)Subsec. (b). , substituted “Armed Forces Retirement Home Trust Fund” for “Soldiers’ Home, Permanent Fund” in two places.Pub. L. 101–510, § 1533(c)(1)(B)1989—Subsec. (b). substituted “annual appropriations and only if the appropriations are specifically authorized by law.” for “annual appropriations. Those appropriations are authorized to be made.”Pub. L. 101–1891984—Subsec. (a)(12). substituted “Longshore” for “Longshoremen’s”.Pub. L. 98–426

A, title IX, § 906(f)(3)112 Stat. 2096Oct. 17, 1998“The amendments made by this subsection [amending this section and repealing , Armed Forces] shall take effect 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”section 2221 of Title 10Oct. 17, 1998

Effective Date of 1989 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–189, div. A, title III, § 341(b)103 Stat. 1419Nov. 29, 1989“The amendments made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply with respect to appropriations for the operation of the United States Soldiers’ and Airmen’s Home [now Armed Forces Retirement Home—Washington] made for fiscal years after fiscal year 1990.”

Effective Date of 1984 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under , Navigation and Navigable Waters.Pub. L. 98–426section 28(e)(1) of Pub. L. 98–426section 901 of Title 33Sept. 28, 1984

Investment of National Park Service Trust Funds, , , provided in part that:Pub. L. 114–113, div. G, title I129 Stat. 2533Dec. 18, 2015“In fiscal year 2016 and each fiscal year thereafter, any amounts deposited into the National Park Service trust fund accounts ((l7)–(18)) shall be invested by the Secretary of the Treasury in interest bearing obligations of the United States to the extent such amounts are not, in his judgment, required to meet current withdrawals: , That interest earned by such investments shall be available for obligation without further appropriation, to the benefit of the project.”31 U.S.C. 1321(a)Provided

Trust Funds for Individual IndiansSection 725s of former Title 31 (now this section) was modified by , , providing that it shall not be applicable to funds held in trust for individual Indians, associations of individual Indians, or for Indian corporations chartered under sections 5101 to 5103, 5107 to 5113, 5115, 5116, 5118, 5120, 5121, 5123 to 5125, and 5129 of Title 25, Indians.act June 25, 1936, ch. 81449 Stat. 1928

Amounts (except amounts received by the Comptroller of the Currency and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation) that are analogous to the funds named in subsection (a) of this section and are received by the United States Government as trustee shall be deposited in an appropriate trust fund account in the Treasury. Except as provided in paragraph (2), amounts accruing to these funds are appropriated to be disbursed in compliance with the terms of the trust.
